state_refinement.note: On top of the merged workspace.bind user path (mcp#706/#707), this change adds git-remote-as-evidence: MCP normalizes the candidate folder's git remote to a stable org/repo ref and uses it only to shape proposal confidence/reason, plus a client-side fail-closed guard that refuses to dispatch when the remote clearly contradicts a supplied registered project source ref. The daemon still owns validation, materialization, and the durable LocalWorkspaceBinding lifecycle; MCP persists no state and adds no new durable lifecycle state.
state_refinement.smallest_honest_release_state: n/a — no durable state added by this client change
invariants: The git remote is evidence only, never project identity; project_id remains the authority key.
A clear remote/project contradiction fails closed at the MCP surface: no workspace.bind request is dispatched and nothing is materialized.
MCP does not persist LocalWorkspaceBinding or BindingEvidence; it only proposes with source_surface=mcp and renders the daemon verdict verbatim.
state_refinement.minimization_review: Canonical state, authority, and persistence semantics did not change; the client remains a transport surface that proposes and renders daemon verdicts verbatim. Git-remote evidence only modulates advisory confidence and adds a pre-dispatch fail-closed guard.
